title: sticherus pubescens
intro: Sticherus pubescens, also known as the hairy rhipsalis, is a type of epiphytic fern that belongs to the family Lindsaeaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Sticherus pubescens include its ability to treat various ailments, such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or ointment.
